title: Race/year-aware grouped validation instead of a single random holdout
summary: Add GroupKFold validation by Race_Year or Race to reduce CV/public mismatch and detect brittle race-context features.
rationale: Single random holdout validation can reward feature families that memorize race, driver, lap phase, or strategy context shared between train and validation rows. Some apparently stronger local variants can fail to generalize, so feature selection needs a stronger internal validation signal than one random split.
implementation_hint: Add a validation harness that evaluates the current feature set with 5-fold GroupKFold by Race_Year when available. Fall back to Race grouping, then to stratified folds if group columns are unavailable. Report mean AUC, standard deviation, worst-fold AUC, and the existing random-holdout AUC for comparison. Use this harness to compare the current feature families without changing submission generation.
expected_effect: Large improvement in experiment selection quality and small to medium direct effect on public score. It should penalize fragile feature families and reduce the risk of optimizing to a random holdout split.
risk: Grouped CV may underestimate performance if the Kaggle test distribution shares race-like structure with the training data. Target encodings and any target-derived statistics must be recomputed fold-safely to avoid leakage.
